behavior_summary: This script implements Malchir, an NPC who runs a scripted Acolyte three‑spell test (Flame Bolt → Explosion → Summon Daemon → Banish) and who can transition to hostile behavior if the player fails or repeats actions incorrectly.

U8P_MALCHIR::cast handles incoming spell targeting on Malchir: if the global test state (MalchirTest) indicates terminal/hostile state (>=6) it routes to an internal handler; otherwise it forces an anger escalation and defers to the spell/combat engine (SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop).

U8P_MALCHIR::use implements player interaction: toggles Malchir's UI gump, begins conversation, sets malchirMet on first meeting and invokes U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2 to start or continue the test, or routes into an angry monologue (U8P_MALCHIR::func090D) when MalchirTest >= 6.

U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2 is the central monologue that explains the test, sets MalchirTest = 1 on start, calls FIRESPEL support and helper routines (U8P_MALCHIR::func0BE7) to stage spell phases and increments malchirMad to escalate warnings. It can ultimately call Npc::setTarget to make Malchir hostile.

U8P_MALCHIR::func09F2 and U8P_MALCHIR::func0BE7 coordinate ceremony/scene steps (closing UI, putting the avatar in stasis, teleporting/positioning, spawning spell effects) and set inDaemonScene to suppress duplicate scene starts.

Scheduling and fast-area handlers (U8P_MALCHIR::schedule, U8P_MALCHIR::enterFastArea, U8P_MALCHIR::leaveFastArea) control NPC positioning, start the ceremony when the avatar enters, and clear references when leaving; they avoid acting while Malchir is dead or if the daemon scene is already active.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MALCHIR::cast: reads MalchirTest and branches — MalchirTest >= 6 → internal ceremony/cleanup handler (U8P_MALCHIR::func034B) and item cleanup; MalchirTest < 6 → set malchirMad = 3 and call SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop (enter hostile combat flow).

U8P_MALCHIR::use → BeginConversation → call U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2; if malchirMet is false U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2 plays music, sets MalchirTest=1 and calls FIRESPEL::func1CEB and U8P_MALCHIR::func0BE7 to start the first phase. If MalchirTest >= 6 U8P_MALCHIR::use routes to U8P_MALCHIR::func090D (hostile branch).

U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2 uses malchirMad as an escalation counter: repeated noncompliance increments malchirMad, producing progressively sterner barks and ultimately calling Npc::setTarget to initiate hostility.

U8P_MALCHIR::func09F2 is a cinematic/scene sequence: sets inDaemonScene, closes gumps, sets avatar stasis, teleports/positions actors and spawns FIRESPEL effects, with suspend points to wait for spawned pids.
U8P_MALCHIR::schedule sets temporary local flags and calls METHOD::func143A; if process_result() is true it calls METHOD::func133F — i.e., scheduling yields then continues based on returned result.
open_questions: What exactly does U8P_MALCHIR::func034B implement (it is called from U8P_MALCHIR::cast when MalchirTest >= 6 but is not present in the decompiled list)?
Precise semantics and side effects of METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7: they are central to ceremony staging but their internal effects are only inferred from usage.
Exact data encoded in the U8P_MALCHIR::cast() uword parameter (param1) — whether it is an identifier of the caster, spell id, or U8P_MALCHIR::cast metadata is not explicit in the script.
Whether Malchir's hostile transition uses any additional global state beyond malchirMad/MalchirTest (e.g., persistent hostility timers) is not visible from this script alone.

main_function.name: U8P_MALCHIR::cast
main_function.purpose: Handle spells U8P_MALCHIR::cast at Malchir: gate spell resolution through ceremony/test state (MalchirTest) and either route to internal ceremony/cleanup handling or escalate to combat/spell resolution (SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop).
entry_conditions: Invoked when Malchir is targeted by a spell (inferred from process name 'U8P_MALCHIR::cast' and call to SPELCMBT::combatSpellLoop).
Behavior depends on global flag MalchirTest (read) and an incoming uword parameter (param1).

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::cast
function_map.role: Entry for spells targeting Malchir; gates between ceremony/hostile behavior and spell-combat engine.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::look
function_map.role: Respond to player 'U8P_MALCHIR::look' by emitting a short description that adapts to malchirName/malchirJob and dead state.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::schedule
function_map.role: Periodic AI scheduling that places or moves Malchir when alive and not in the daemon scene.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::enterFastArea
function_map.role: Trigger when an actor enters Malchir's trigger area; may start the ceremony (METHOD::func15E7) or set target when avatarMasterSorcerer is true.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::leaveFastArea
function_map.role: Cleanup on actor exit: clear references (FREE::resetRef).

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::use
function_map.role: Handle player interaction: open/close gump, start conversation and the Acolyte test via U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2, set malchirMet on first meet, or route to hostile monologue.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::func03F2
function_map.role: Primary monologue and test driver: explain test steps, initialize MalchirTest, call spell prep and ceremony helpers, increment malchirMad on noncompliance and potentially trigger hostility.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::func090D
function_map.role: Angry/hostile monologue and transition to combat: destroy test artifacts, set malchirMad and set combat target (Npc::setTarget).

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::func09F2
function_map.role: Ceremony/cinematic sequence: close UI, set avatar stasis, teleport/position actors, spawn effects and set inDaemonScene.

function_map.name: U8P_MALCHIR::func0BE7
function_map.role: Mid-test utility: conditionally call METHOD::func091C to advance the ceremony or call U8P_MALCHIR::func09F2 when MalchirTest indicates a terminal state.

internal_calls.purpose: Handle U8P_MALCHIR::cast event when MalchirTest >= 6 (internal ceremony/cleanup path).
Route the U8P_MALCHIR::cast into the spell/combat engine when the test is not in a terminal state (MalchirTest < 6).
Begin or continue the Acolyte test monologue and sequencing on player interaction.
Advance ceremony steps after initializing a test phase.
Call terminal ceremony/scene handler when MalchirTest >= 6 to perform staging/cleanup/hostile transition.
Seen in the broader flow as part of multi-step ceremony sequencing (mutual U8P_MALCHIR::use across steps).

external_calls.purpose: Execute spell combat resolution when Malchir is attacked during non-terminal test state.
Prepare/trigger the Flame Bolt phase when starting the test.
Primary movement/AI placement call during scheduling.
Follow-up movement/AI action invoked when process_result() returns true after suspension.
Start or advance the daemon/ceremony scene when an actor enters Malchir's area and not already in the daemon scene.
Assign Malchir a combat target when avatarMasterSorcerer is true (special-case behavior).
Clear references to Malchir when an actor leaves the fast area (cleanup).
Determine whether Malchir's gump is open to decide between closing or opening it.
Close Malchir's UI gump if it is open.
Open Malchir's UI gump (gump id 0x0C) for interaction.
Play background/test music at the start of the Acolyte test (playMusic(0x7A)).
Ensure the UI is cleared before starting cinematic ceremony steps.
Immobilize the avatar during the ceremony to control the cinematic.
Spawn fire/spell visual effects at computed coordinates for ceremony visuals.
Advance mid-test ceremony steps when MalchirTest is in a non-terminal range.
Destroy/cleanup an item referenced by local1 in the hostile/cleanup path.
Center the camera during cinematic staging and restore it afterwards.
Teleport NPC(s) to staged scene positions for the ceremony.

reads.name: malchirName
reads.likely_effect: Select whether name-based descriptions are used in U8P_MALCHIR::look text.

reads.name: malchirJob
reads.likely_effect: Select whether job-based descriptions are used in U8P_MALCHIR::look text.

reads.name: Npc::isDead(this)
reads.likely_effect: Guard: suppress dialog, scheduling, and scene starts if Malchir is dead.

reads.name: malchirMet
reads.likely_effect: Detect first meeting to play the introductory monologue; prevents repeating the full intro.

reads.name: MalchirTest
reads.likely_effect: Primary test-state machine value that gates whether the Acolyte test continues or Malchir enters hostile/terminal behavior (threshold checks against values like 1–5 and >=6).

reads.name: malchirMad
reads.likely_effect: Escalation counter for warnings/anger that influences dialog branches and triggers hostility when incremented sufficiently.

reads.name: avatarMasterSorcerer
reads.likely_effect: When true, change U8P_MALCHIR::enterFastArea behavior to force Malchir to target the avatar (special privileges).

reads.name: inDaemonScene
reads.likely_effect: Guard to prevent re-entry/duplication of the daemon/ceremony cinematic; when true many scene-start actions are suppressed.

writes.name: malchirMad
writes.likely_effect: Incremented or set to escalate Malchir's warnings and eventually force hostile behavior (e.g., set to 3 in some paths).

writes.name: malchirMet
writes.likely_effect: Set on first interaction to indicate the player has met Malchir and prevent repeating the full intro monologue.

writes.name: MalchirTest
writes.likely_effect: Set to 1 when the test begins and read subsequently to control required player actions and transitions; used to advance or terminate the ceremony.

writes.name: inDaemonScene
writes.likely_effect: Set to true when the ceremony cinematic starts to prevent duplicate scene starts; cleared elsewhere when the ceremony ends (clearing not shown in this script).

writes.name: local0
writes.likely_effect: Temporary flag written by U8P_MALCHIR::func0BE7 to manage internal sequencing; scope appears local to the function but persists across suspend points.

writes.name: local1/local2/local3 (schedule)
writes.likely_effect: Temporary U8P_MALCHIR::schedule flags set by U8P_MALCHIR::schedule to drive METHOD::func143A behavior (movement/positioning parameters).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements Malchir, an NPC who runs a scripted Acolyte three‑spell test (Flame Bolt → Explosion → Summon Daemon → Banish) and who can transition to hostile behavior if the player fails or repeats actions incorrectly.
